howItWorks.intro: This food storage calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Calories = People × Days × Calories per Day
Weight (lbs) = Total Calories ÷ Calories per Pound
Cost = Total Calories × Cost per Calorie
Bulk staples average 1,600 cal/lb; canned goods ~600 cal/lb
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much food should I store for emergencies?
faq.answer: FEMA recommends a minimum 3-day supply. Many preppers target 30-90 days. A 30-day supply for a family of 4 requires about 240,000 calories — roughly 240 lbs of mixed food.

faq.question: What are the best foods for long-term storage?
faq.answer: Best shelf life: white rice (25+ years), dried beans (25+ years), freeze-dried foods (25+ years), honey (indefinite), powdered milk (25 years). Store in cool, dark, dry conditions.

faq.question: How many calories per day do I need in survival?
faq.answer: Minimum survival: 1,200 calories. Sedentary adult: 1,600-2,000. Active/physical labor: 2,500-3,500. In cold weather, increase by 500-1,000 calories for body heat generation.
